summary: Explosive Scarlet>Fire, monster Cumberland Blues jam, Jerry on fire
blurb: The set bursts open with a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain sequence and a massive Cumberland Blues jam. Jerry’s lead work is intense and urgent while Brent locks in tight keyboard lines and Phil provides a thunderous bass foundation.
review: April 20, 1984 at the Philadelphia Civic Arena captures one of the spring‑’84 high points for the Dead. The first set rolls through classics like Feel Like A Stranger, Cold Rain And Snow and a bright Let It Grow before the band launches into an unforgettable second‑set centerpiece: Scarlet Begonias segues seamlessly into Fire On The Mountain, driving the crowd into a frenzy that only Jerry’s “on fire” lead work could sustain. From there the momentum never wanes – a thunderous Cumberland Blues jam stretches out with relentless energy, and the set rounds off with high‑octane Chuck Berry covers that keep the vibe upbeat to the encore. Across every source – pristine soundboard FLACs, a crystal audience tape, and even a superb matrix capture – the performance shines: Jerry’s solos are described as “intense, urgent, aggressive” and even “wailing like a bleeding goat,” while Phil’s bass is praised for “bombing the stage” with his new Modulus tone. Brent’s keyboards weave melodic support that locks step with Jerry on Stranger and the Scarlet>Fire sequence, and the dual drummers provide a solid, thumping rhythmic foundation throughout.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all recordings: multiple reviewers call the Scarlet>Fire sequence "scorching" and Jerry’s guitar "on fire," praise a "monster Cumberland" jam, and note tight keyboard work and powerful bass. No consistent negative comments about performance; only one minor poor‑song mention (Morning Dew).

best_recording.reason: Excellent FLAC soundboard capture with crisp, bright mix; highlights the full energy of the show and preserves every instrument detail.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue
Monster Cumberland Blues jam that drives the second set
Jerry’s intense lead work throughout, especially on Feel Like A Stranger and Scarlet>Fire
Brent’s tight, melodic keyboard support and Phil’s powerful bass foundation

band_performance.Jerry: Intense, urgent and aggressive lead work; solos described as "on fire" and "wailing like a bleeding goat," especially powerful during Scarlet>Fire and Feel Like A Stranger.
band_performance.Phil: Bombs the stage with a thunderous bass foundation; new Modulus tone adds rumble and depth, earning frequent "Phil Bomb" mentions.
band_performance.Bob: Shows improved slide playing and chiming at right moments, adding texture to the set.
band_performance.Brent: Locks step with Jerry, delivering tight, melodic support; praised for polished, prime‑time keyboards that shine on Scarlet>Fire and Stranger.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid, thumping drum work alongside Mickey; drums are noted as a strong rhythmic foundation throughout.
